title: Chef and Queries on a Tree

content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tree with NNN nodes numbered 111 through NNN. Each node of the tree has a weight; let's denote the weight of node vvv by ava_vav​.

Chef should answer QQQ queries. Each query is described by an integer rrr and a list of kkk nodes u1,u2,…,uku_1, u_2, \dots, u_ku1​,u2​,…,uk​. In each query, let SSS be the connected subgraph of the tree (i.e. a subtree) which contains the nodes u1,u2,…,uku_1, u_2, \dots, u_ku1​,u2​,…,uk​ and has the minimum possible size (it can be proven that these conditions uniquely define SSS). The answer to a query is minu∈S(∣au−r∣)\mathrm{min}_{u \in S}(|a_u - r|)minu∈S​(∣au​−r∣).

Help Chef and answer all the queries. Note the special format of the input, which is intended to ensure you can only know each query (except the first) after answering the previous query.
constraints: The graph described on the input is a tree
In each query, u1, u2, …, uk are pairwise distinct
The sum of k for each test case does not exceed 3*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and Q. The second line contains N space-separated integers a1, a2, …, aN. Each of the following N-1 lines contains two space-separated integers x and y denoting an edge between nodes x and y. The following Q lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers r′ and k, followed by a space and k space-separated integers u1′, u2′, …, uk′.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer — the answer to this query.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a combination of graph traversal algorithms such as BFS or DFS to find the connected subgraph of the tree that contains the given nodes and has the minimum possible size. We can then iterate over the nodes in this subgraph to find the node with the minimum absolute difference from the given value r.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N + Q * k)) — where T is the number of test cases, N is the number of nodes in the tree, Q is the number of queries, and k is the maximum number of nodes in a query.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N + Q * k) — where N is the number of nodes in the tree and Q * k is the maximum number of nodes in all queries.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, Q = map(int, input().split())
    a = [0] + list(map(int, input().split()))
    # Read the tree edges
    for _ in range(N - 1):
        x, y = map(int, input().split())
        # Add edge to the tree
    ans = 0
    for _ in range(Q):
        r, k = map(int, input().split())
        u = list(map(int, input().split()))
        # XOR the values
        for i in range(k):
            u[i] ^= ans
        r ^= ans
        ans = min(abs(a[i] - r) for i in u)
        print(ans)
